description: Computes the analytic envelope via Hilbert-like approximation (abs of signal after subtracting a 15-sample moving average to remove DC). The envelope is then smoothed with an exponential moving average (alpha=0.15). An adaptive baseline tracks the 20th percentile of the envelope over a 256-sample history, and the score is the ratio of current envelope to adaptive baseline. This makes the detector self-calibrating to changing impedance.
notes: Self-calibrating to impedance drift via adaptive baseline. The DC-removal step prevents slow electrode polarization from inflating the envelope. EMA smoothing reduces jitter in the envelope estimate. Lower thresholds than spike model because the envelope ratio is already normalized.
